title: Example 2.5 Impulse Response via Impulse Matching
summary: This page explains why the impulse response of a continuous-time LTI system consists of characteristic mode terms for t > 0 and may include an impulse term at t = 0. It derives that the impulse coefficient equals b0 by matching impulsive terms and then begins Example 2.5 on finding h(t) via impulse matching for a second-order differential equation.
